<!-- copilotide Session c7a0ad6c-c719-4339-a07a-abc0844a570b (2026-03-21 02:39:06Z) -->

_**User (2026-03-21 02:39:06Z)**_

How do I check which device the model weights is on? 

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

To check which device your model weights are on, you can use these approaches:

**1. Quick check of a specific parameter:**
```python
print(surrogate_model.W_E.device)  # Check embedding weights device
```

**2. Check all parameters:**
```python
for name, param in surrogate_model.named_parameters():
    print(f"{name}: {param.device}")
```

**3. Get the device of the first parameter (most common):**
```python
next(surrogate_model.parameters()).device
```

**4. Check after forward pass (see where activations end up):**
```python
with t.inference_mode():
    logits = surrogate_model("hello")
print(logits.device)
```

In your case, since you loaded the model with `device=device` (which you set to MPS, CUDA, or CPU), the weights should already be on your selected device. You can verify this quickly with:

```python
print(next(surrogate_model.parameters()).device)
```

This will print something like `device(type='cuda', index=0)`, `device(type='mps')`, or `device(type='cpu')` depending on where your model is actually loaded.
